summary: Recent changes in the Media IPC (Inter-Process Communication) area of Firefox enhance how the browser handles digital rights management (DRM) and video encoding technologies. By adjusting the lifetime management of the Content Decryption Module (CDM), which is crucial for securely playing DRM-protected content, Firefox improves the efficiency of video playback. Additionally, filtering hardware and software support in the video encoding process based on user preferences ensures that the browser can optimize performance, whether users choose to rely on hardware acceleration or software, thus making video streaming smoother and more adaptable to different system capabilities. These updates are part of a broader effort to enhance user experience and maintain robust DRM support.
